description: DRAFT, non-normative (RUN-ATTEST-03 B.2). A scoped vouch: a unilateral claim by the record author about another persona, standing on at least one resolvable qualifying antecedent from the CLOSED antecedent class (V1 / OC-2 DECIDED: co_signed_edge | transaction | ceremony). Zero qualifying antecedents folds to pending (T-A3.2). No numeric score field exists (T-AT0.2 as schema absence). Withdrawal is author-sovereign record deletion + Drystone supersede lineage (V2), not a record kind.

scope.description: Named scope, matched by EXACT equality in queries — never fuzzy, never a category tree (T-AT3.2).

statement.description: Asserted claim text; the protocol never evaluates it.

baseEdge.description: Canonical core hash of the base edge, present only for an edge-based vouch (V1: optional; an edge-free vouch stands on transaction/ceremony antecedents).

antecedents.description: The qualifying-antecedent citations. Standing requires at least one to resolve as its claimed kind.

supersedes.description: Narrowing/replacement lineage pointer (supersede, never revoke — T-AT0.3).

kind.description: The CLOSED class (crate: AntecedentKind). Lexicon enum, never knownValues — the compile boundary expressed at the schema layer; which kinds QUALIFY is additionally the R7-governed register (T-A3.4).
